updates.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Reflect — An AI Usage Analytics Dashboard
updates.summary: Anthropic introduced 'Reflect', a built-in analytics dashboard that lets Claude users visualise how they use the platform over the past 1, 3, 6, or 12 months — covering topics discussed, usage patterns, most active days, and peak hours. The dashboard also includes wellbeing features: periodic reflection prompts ('What's one thing you want to keep doing yourself, even if Claude could do it faster?'), quiet hours settings, and usage-break nudges. Available in beta for Free, Pro, and Max users with memory turned on, via the Claude web client and desktop app.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via official Anthropic blog (anthropic.com/news/reflect-with-claude), independently corroborated by Engadget and TechCrunch. Feature details align across sources. No contested claims.

updates.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web — 90%+ of Usage Isn't Coding
updates.summary: Anthropic announced that Claude Cowork is expanding from the desktop app to mobile and web, with a gradual beta rollout starting with Max plan users. New capabilities include cross-device session sync (start at desk, monitor from phone, retrieve output from any device), background task execution with no device online, and scheduled-work automation. Accompanying usage data revealed that more than 90% of Cowork usage is non-coding: business operations and process account for 33.4%, making it the largest category. Anthropic extended doubled Cowork usage limits through August 5 to mark the rollout. Microsoft 365 write-tool integration (email, calendar, OneDrive/SharePoint) is also included.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via official Anthropic blog, corroborated by TechCrunch (2026-07-07), 9to5Mac, MacRumors, and VentureBeat. Usage statistics (90%+ non-coding, 33.4% business ops) cited from Anthropic's own data. Rollout timeline (weeks, Max plan first) consistent across sources.

updates.title: Claude Code and Claude Cowork Launch in Public Beta for Government Desktop (FedRAMP High)
updates.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Code and Claude Cowork inside its Claude for Government Desktop offering, achieving FedRAMP High authorization — the most stringent classification for handling unclassified sensitive government data. The beta brings desktop file-based work, stronger admin controls, tamper-evident audit logs, and spend governance to public-sector teams. Anthropic is extending Claude for Enterprise and Claude for Government access to all three branches of the US federal government (executive, legislative, judiciary) and is partnering with AWS, Google Cloud, and Palantir for secure infrastructure delivery.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official news page. FedRAMP High certification status verified against prior related Anthropic announcements about Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud Vertex AI FedRAMP High approvals. Government-specific features described are consistent with enterprise Claude rollouts. No contested claims identified.

updates.title: Anthropic Expands Agent Capabilities API: Code Execution, MCP Connector, Files API & 1-Hour Prompt Caching
updates.summary: Anthropic announced a set of new capabilities on the Claude API designed for agent builders. The code execution tool lets Claude run Python in a sandboxed REPL with persistent state, enabling data analysis and visualisation without needing external infrastructure. The MCP connector allows developers to connect Claude to any remote Model Context Protocol server without writing client code — Anthropic handles connection management, tool discovery, and error handling. The Files API enables persistent file storage and cross-session access. Prompt caching now extends to one hour. Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, Ruby, PHP, and C# SDKs all received support for these new capabilities.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official news and corroborated by search results citing releasebot.io July 2026 entries. Code execution, MCP connector, and prompt caching specifics match the official docs.anthropic.com release notes language. SDK language coverage list confirmed. No contested claims.

updates.title: I Used Claude Fable 5 for Zero-Shot Coding — and Understood Why Anthropic Locked It Down
updates.summary: XDA tested Claude Fable 5's zero-shot coding capabilities after its redeployment on July 1, following a three-week suspension due to US Department of Commerce export controls. The review found that the redeployed Fable 5 performs closer to Opus 4.8 than to the model's initial launch benchmarks, suggesting Anthropic applied safety refinements during the suspension period. The author concludes that Fable 5 remains excellent for zero-shot coding tasks but does not represent the generational leap over Opus 4.8 that early reviews had anticipated, broadly consistent with other post-redeployment reports.
updates.accuracy_notes: Publication date confirmed as July 8, 2026 ('1 day ago' in search metadata). XDA's findings align with other independent reports that the redeployed Fable 5 feels closer to Opus 4.8. The claim that Anthropic applied safety refinements is the author's inference, not an Anthropic statement — flag this as analytical rather than confirmed. Fable 5 launch (June 9), suspension (June 12), and redeployment (July 1) dates verified across multiple sources.

updates.title: I Set Up Claude Code the Way Anthropic Now Recommends — The Sub-Agents-Spawning-Sub-Agents Trick Changed How I Work
updates.summary: XDA covers the Claude Code v2.1.172 update that enabled subagents to spawn their own subagents — hierarchical delegation that previously stopped one level deep. Anthropic's canonical example: a reviewer subagent that reads a diff, produces findings, then fans out a separate verifier subagent per finding, returning only the top-level summary. The article also recommends running 3-5 parallel Claude Code sessions in isolated git worktrees as the biggest workflow productivity multiplier. Previously, subagents could receive tasks but could not themselves delegate further, creating a bottleneck at each level of agentic decomposition.
updates.accuracy_notes: Publication date confirmed as July 7, 2026 ('2 days ago' in search metadata). Claude Code v2.1.172 version number cited — verify against official Claude Code changelog (docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes/claude-code) if critical. Hierarchical subagent capability is consistent with the Claude Agent SDK architecture described in Anthropic's official documentation. The depth cap on nesting is mentioned but not quantified; treat as accurate-but-unverified until confirmed in changelog.
